message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This note provides an assessment of COVID-19 acceleration among groups with different vaccine status in France.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We assess viral acceleration using a novel indicator introduced in Baunez et al. (2021). The acceleration index relates the percentage change of tests that have been performed on a given day to the percentage change in the associated positive cases that same day. We compare viral acceleration among vaccinated and unvaccinated individuals in France over the period May 31st - August 29, 2021.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Once the state of the epidemic within each groups is accounted for, it turns out that viral
                    <jats:italic>acceleration</jats:italic>
                    has since mid-July converged to similar levels among vaccinated and unvaccinated individuals in France, even though viral
                    <jats:italic>speed</jats:italic>
                    is larger for the latter group compared to the former.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Our results call for an increasing testing effort for
                    <jats:italic>both</jats:italic>
                    vaccinated and unvaccinated individuals, in view of the fact that viral circulation is currently accelerating at similar levels for both groups in France.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: COVID-19 Acceleration and Vaccine Status in France - Summer 2021

reference.unstructured: Baunez C. , Degoulet M. , Luchini S. , Pintus P. , Teschl M. (2021): Tracking the Dynamics and Allocating Tests for COVID-19 in Real-Time: an Acceleration Index with an Application to French Age Groups and Départements. PLoS ONE, June 1st, available at https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0252443. 3, 4
